spec_match.rationale: Codemod that rewrites `.calendars`, `.weekInfo`, `.timeFormats`, etc. to the `.getCalendars()`, `.getWeekInfo()`, `.getTimeFormats()` method form — exact migration the deprecation requires.

interactive.rationale: Source textarea + sample-loader + rewrite + findings list + copy-to-clipboard. Six controls, real codemod behaviour.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Index promises 'paste any source file; rewrites each legacy getter into the new method form, lists every hit, and lets you copy the patched output.' Delivered.

edge_cases.rationale: Masks string literals before matching (good for skipping the 'user's calendars are:' comment string in the sample). But the regex `(\.)(${p})\b(?!\s*\()` will rewrite ANY object's `.calendars` access — including `obj.calendars[0]` in the sample where `obj` is not an Intl.Locale. The page concedes that line shouldn't change but the codemod still rewrites it; the visible findings list will include false positives.

references.rationale: See-also includes https://chromestatus.com/feature/5148228059398144 and the TC39 PR that landed the rename.

design_system.rationale: Variables-only; .ok / .dirty findings cells use accent colours.

openQuestions.title: Use a real JS parser instead of regex
openQuestions.detail: The regex matches any `.calendars` access whether or not the receiver is an Intl.Locale. The sample even includes `obj.calendars[0]` as a counterexample but the page rewrites it anyway. Parse the source with a real JS parser (acorn or @babel/parser) and only rewrite member expressions whose receiver is type-able to Intl.Locale. Codemods that produce false positives are worse than no codemod.

openQuestions.title: Add the `getTimeFormats()` row to the matrix sibling
openQuestions.detail: This codemod includes `timeFormats` in its props array but the getter-vs-getter matrix omits it. Either delete `timeFormats` here (if it never shipped) or add it everywhere — currently the two pages disagree.

openQuestions.title: Show a diff view instead of two textareas
openQuestions.detail: A line-by-line diff (red strike-through on the old form, green underline on the new form) would let visitors verify the codemod's hits without manual scanning. The findings list is already populated with line + name — drive a diff renderer from that.

openQuestions.title: Bundle as a npm-installable codemod
openQuestions.detail: The page would be much more useful if it also shipped as `npx @paul/intl-locale-info-codemod ./src` for use in CI. Linking to such a package (and providing the AST-based version of the regex from #1) would let teams adopt this without manual paste.

summary: Useful one-page codemod, but the regex-based rewrite produces false positives (admitted by the sample's own `obj.calendars[0]` counterexample). Replacing the regex with a real JS parser is the major fix; adding a diff view and aligning the props list with the matrix sibling is the rest.
